ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for Salmon Kal Varuval specifically; closest parent is the general salmon food product class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Salmon, cooked) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code exists for Indian spiced grilled salmon.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Salmon Kal Varuval or a sufficiently close Indian spiced fish preparation.

serving.description: 1 portion (approximately 1 salmon fillet with spice crust)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Salmon Kal Varuval is one salmon fillet of approximately 200 g, served as a main course with rice or bread.

nutrient_source.title: Atlantic salmon, cooked, dry heat — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 15076); adapted with spice-marinade adjustment for Indian Kal Varuval preparation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Base nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for Atlantic salmon cooked by dry heat (FDC ID 175167). Sodium adjusted upward to reflect spice marinade (ginger-garlic paste, salt, chilli) typical of Kal Varuval preparation. Fat adjusted slightly upward for cooking oil used on hot plate. Carbohydrate and sugar values reflect minor contribution from spice paste. Values are consistent with ICMR-NIN Indian Food Composition Tables 2017 entry for cooked fish with spices.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate for plain cooked fish (GI ~0–5)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Salmon Kal Varuval. GI is estimated at 5 based on the established principle that protein- and fat-dominant fish dishes with negligible starch have a very low glycemic index, consistent with University of Sydney GI Database entries for cooked fish and meat dishes (GI range 0–10). The small carbohydrate contribution (~2.8 g/100 g) comes from spice paste and has negligible glycemic impact. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (5 × 5.6) / 100 = 0.28, rounded to 0.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from spice marinade and salt
hypertension.guidance: Salmon Kal Varuval contains approximately 420 mg sodium per 100 g due to the salt-heavy spice marinade; individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100–150 g and request reduced-salt preparation where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low glycemic index and high protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 5 and only 2.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g, Salmon Kal Varuval has a negligible glycemic load and is a suitable high-protein, low-GI option for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, provided sodium intake is monitored.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids from salmon
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Salmon is a rich source of long-chain omega-3 fatty acids (EPA and DHA), with approximately 3.6 g polyunsaturated fat per 100 g; regular consumption of salmon preparations such as Kal Varuval may support cardiovascular health, though the moderate sodium content warrants attention in patients with heart failure or uncontrolled hypertension.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salmon Kal Varuval is a South Indian dry-fried fish dish in which salmon fillets are marinated in a robust spice paste comprising ginger-garlic paste, green chilli, turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin, and curry leaves, then cooked on a hot iron griddle or tawa with a small amount of oil until the exterior forms a deeply caramelised, aromatic crust while the interior remains moist and flaky. The term 'Kal' refers to the stone or iron surface traditionally used for cooking, imparting a characteristic char and smoky depth. The dish is intensely savoury and spiced, with a firm, slightly crisp outer texture contrasting with the rich, oily flesh of the salmon. Nutritionally, Salmon Kal Varuval is a high-protein (approximately 22 g per 100 g), moderate-fat dish rich in omega-3 polyunsaturated fatty acids, vitamin D, vitamin B12, selenium, and niacin. Its glycemic index is very low (estimated GI ~5), making it highly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The sodium content is moderate to high due to the spice marinade, which is a consideration for individuals with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. In Singapore, this dish is served at South Indian restaurants and is popular as a main course alongside rice, rasam, or parotta. It is halal-certified in most South Indian Muslim establishments and is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free.
common_user_queries: Is Salmon Kal Varuval good for diabetics?
What spices are used in Kal Varuval?
Salmon Kal Varuval vs salmon curry
How is Kal Varuval different from regular fish fry?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Salmon Kal Varuval exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN tables. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy Atlantic salmon (cooked, dry heat) with adjustments for spice marinade and cooking oil, cross-referenced against ICMR-NIN 2017 cooked fish entries. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database principles for protein-dominant fish dishes. Confidence is medium rather than low because the base ingredient (cooked salmon) is well-characterised in approved sources.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 175167, Atlantic salmon cooked dry heat) with upward sodium adjustment (~420 mg/100 g) to reflect salt in spice marinade, and minor fat adjustment for cooking oil. 2. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all reported as positive numeric values derived from USDA SR Legacy salmon data; no sentinel -1 values are used in this record. 3. GI estimated at 5 (effectively negligible) based on University of Sydney GI Database analogous fish dish data; no direct GI measurement for Salmon Kal Varuval exists in the literature. 4. Trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in cooked salmon per USDA data. 5. The dish is served at Manam - Upper Circular Road, Singapore, at SGD 33.00. 6. Halal status is assumed based on typical South Indian Muslim restaurant practice; individual restaurant certification should be verified. 7. FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code are closest-parent matches; no exact ontology code exists for this specific preparation.
